feature: Agent ID CLI + Registration Lifecycle Completion
description: Add `hex agent id` command for self-discovery, fix `hex agent connect` to write session files and send project_dir, and unify all resolve_agent_id() call sites to the canonical 4-strategy implementation. Closes remaining gaps from ADR-065 phases P1–P3.

phases.name: Add `hex agent id` CLI subcommand
phases.description: Add an `Id` variant to AgentAction that takes no arguments. It calls the canonical `nexus_client::read_session_agent_id()` and prints the resolved agent ID, resolution method used, and session file path. If resolution fails, print a clear error with hints (run `hex agent connect` or check CLAUDE_SESSION_ID). This is the 'who am I?' command.

tasks.name: Add `hex agent id` subcommand to AgentAction enum and dispatch
tasks.description: Add `Id` variant (no args) to the AgentAction enum in agent.rs. Wire it in the `run()` match. Implement `agent_id()` fn that calls `crate::nexus_client::read_session_agent_id()`. On success: print agent ID (full UUID), short ID (8-char), resolution method (env/pid/file), and session file path. On None: print error with hints — 'Could not resolve agent ID. Try: hex agent connect <nexus-url>, or set HEX_AGENT_ID env var'. Optionally if nexus is reachable, also fetch and print agent status (name, project, last heartbeat) via GET /api/hex-agents/{id}.

phases.name: Fix `hex agent connect` to write session file
phases.description: After successful POST /api/hex-agents/connect, write a session file to ~/.hex/sessions/agent-connect-{generated_session_id}.json with the same schema as session-start hook files. Send project_dir (CWD) and a generated session_id in the POST body. Include projectId from response in the session file.

tasks.name: Send project_dir and session_id in connect POST body
tasks.description: In connect() fn: generate a session_id (format: 'connect-{unix_timestamp}'). Get CWD as project_dir. Add both to the POST /api/hex-agents/connect JSON body alongside existing host and capabilities fields.

tasks.name: Write session file after successful connect
tasks.description: After successful connect response, write ~/.hex/sessions/agent-connect-{session_id}.json. File must contain: agentId (from response), name (hostname), project (from response projectId or project_dir basename), registered_at, session_id, project_dir (CWD). Create ~/.hex/sessions/ dir if missing. Use atomic write (write to .tmp then rename).

phases.name: Unify resolve_agent_id across all call sites
phases.description: Replace the duplicate resolve_agent_id() in inbox.rs with a call to the canonical nexus_client::read_session_agent_id(). Verify test.rs and mcp.rs already use the canonical version. After this phase, there is exactly ONE implementation of agent ID resolution.

tasks.name: Replace inbox.rs resolve_agent_id with canonical call
tasks.description: Delete the local resolve_agent_id() function in inbox.rs (lines 188-224). Replace both call sites (lines 63, 166) with crate::nexus_client::read_session_agent_id().ok_or_else(|| anyhow::anyhow!("Cannot resolve agent ID...")). Keep the error message helpful.

tasks.name: Audit remaining resolve_agent_id call sites
tasks.description: Grep for resolve_agent_id and read_session_agent_id across hex-cli/src/. Verify mcp.rs:583 and test.rs:209-210 already delegate to nexus_client. If any other duplicates exist, replace them. Document the single canonical path in a code comment on read_session_agent_id().

phases.name: Build + Validate
phases.description: Build hex-cli. Verify `hex agent id` works in three scenarios: (1) with CLAUDE_SESSION_ID set, (2) with existing session files, (3) with no session files (error path). Verify `hex agent connect` writes session file. Verify `hex inbox list` uses canonical resolution.

tasks.name: Build and smoke test
tasks.description: cargo build -p hex-cli --release. Run: (1) hex agent id — should resolve from session files or show helpful error; (2) hex agent connect http://localhost:5555 — verify session file written to ~/.hex/sessions/; (3) hex agent id again — should now show the connect session; (4) hex inbox list — should use same agent ID as hex agent id.
